data.title: Asian Markets See Significant Gains as Nikkei and KOSPI Rise
data.description: According to Odaily, the Nikkei 225 index closed on January 13, a Tuesday, with a significant increase of 1,609.27 points, marking a 3.10% rise to reach 53,549.16 points. Meanwhile, South Korea's KOSPI index also experienced an uptick, closing up by 67.84 points, or 1.47%, at 4,692.63 points.

data.title: CZ: I'm not against Meme coin, but blindly following the trend carries the risk of losses.
data.description: CZ posted on the X platform that he is not against Meme coins and likes Meme culture. However, he stated that if retail investors blindly buy every newly created Meme coin based on his random tweets, they will almost certainly suffer financial losses. CZ further stated that his tweets usually only contain some unfunny jokes and that he doesn't spend most of his time thinking about Memes.

data.title: Bitcoin's Role in 401(k) Plans Sparks Debate Amid Volatility Concerns
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Bitwise chief investment officer Matt Hougan has criticized the notion that Bitcoin should be excluded from investment and 401(k) plans due to its volatility, arguing that certain stocks experience even greater price fluctuations. Hougan's remarks coincided with U.S. Senator Elizabeth Warren's inquiry to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) regarding the mitigation of risks associated with including cryptocurrencies in retirement funds.

In August last year, U.S. President Donald Trump issued an executive order instructing the Labor Department to reassess restrictions on alternative assets in defined-contribution plans, paving the way for cryptocurrencies to be incorporated into 401(k) retirement plans. During an interview with Investopedia Express Live, Hougan criticized previous efforts by management companies like Vanguard and regulatory advice against Bitcoin investment in 401(k)s as "ridiculous." He emphasized that Bitcoin, while volatile, has been less so over the past year compared to Nvidia stock, which has not faced similar investment restrictions.

Shares of Nvidia, a major U.S. tech company, experienced a significant price swing, hitting a yearly low of approximately $94.31 in April 2025 and surging to over $207 by October, marking a 120% fluctuation. In contrast, Bitcoin's price varied between a low of $76,000 in April and a high of $126,080 in October, resulting in a 65% swing. The inclusion of crypto in 401(k)s has been a long-standing goal for crypto firms seeking to expand their reach among retail investors and enhance their legitimacy within the financial system.

Meanwhile, U.S. Senator Elizabeth Warren is seeking answers from the SEC on how it plans to address risks for 401(k) plans opting to invest in "alternative investments" like crypto. In an open letter, Warren expressed concerns that crypto in retirement plans might not yield better outcomes for participants due to higher fees and expenses, coupled with crypto's volatility. She emphasized that for most Americans, their 401(k) represents a crucial component of retirement security rather than a venue for financial risk, warning that allowing crypto into retirement accounts could lead to significant losses for workers and families.

Warren has requested that SEC Chair Paul Atkins provide clarity on whether the regulator considers volatility when valuing crypto holdings for publicly traded companies by January 27. She also seeks information on whether the SEC has evaluated manipulative practices in crypto markets and if it will publish research and educational materials to enhance investor awareness.

Outside of Trump's executive order, the Department of Labor’s Employee Benefits Security Administration announced a "neutral stance" on crypto in 401(k)s in May, following the rescission of a 2022 compliance release that had previously discouraged the practice. Hougan noted that while it remains uncertain if 401(k) providers will begin investing in crypto by 2026, he anticipates that it will eventually become normalized, akin to other assets.

data.title: Uniswap Founder Criticizes Celebrity Blockchain Scams
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on January 13, Uniswap founder Hayden Adams commented on the NYC token incident involving former New York City Mayor Eric Adams, strongly criticizing celebrities and politicians for using blockchain technology to perpetrate scams. Adams highlighted that celebrities can legitimately profit from blockchain by issuing tokens while maintaining liquidity, providing real value to holders, and ensuring project transparency.

He emphasized that blockchain is an "unprecedentedly powerful tool for collaboration, monetization, and value distribution," currently utilized by millions worldwide. However, he noted that celebrities often exploit it for short-sighted fraudulent activities. Adams believes that by honestly managing blockchain projects, celebrities can not only achieve greater profits but also safeguard their reputations and avoid legal issues.

data.title: New Crypto Market Structure Draft Prohibits Interest Payments on Stablecoin Balances
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, SolanaFloor shared on the X platform that the latest draft of the crypto market structure has adopted a method for handling stablecoin yields that banks have been advocating. The draft prohibits the payment of interest solely for holding a balance.

Rewards linked to activities such as trading, staking, liquidity provision, or governance participation are still permitted.

data.title: NYC Token: We have noticed the reports of "liquidity removal," which is a liquidity rebalancing based on market demand.
data.description: In response to the rapid rise and fall of NYC Token after its opening, which attracted community attention, the project issued a statement on the X platform to clarify that due to the unexpected market support and demand in the early stages of the token's launch, NYC Token's partners conducted a liquidity rebalancing operation on the liquidity pool. The team has noticed the community reports regarding some "liquidity removal" and clarified that the relevant funds have been reused for project advancement and to add funds to the liquidity pool. The team also emphasized that it will continue to invest in the project's development in the long term.

data.title: The draft crypto market structure prohibits paying interest on stablecoin balances.
data.description: SolanaFloor stated in a post on the X platform that the latest draft of the crypto market structure adopts the stablecoin yield processing method that banks have been pushing for, prohibiting the payment of interest solely for holding. Rewards linked to activities such as trading, staking, liquidity provision, or governance participation are still permitted.

data.title: Court temporarily stops Tennessee from taking action against Kalshi
data.description:
Kalshi is free to continue operating in Tennessee for now, as a federal judge blocked state regulators from taking action after the prediction market sued.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/court-temporarily-stops-tennessee-taking-action-against-kalshi?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Gate Alpha is launching its 143rd points airdrop, where holders of the corresponding points can be among the first to claim 0.45, 0.95, or 1.95 GT.
data.description: According to official news, Gate Alpha will launch its 143rd GT points airdrop on January 13th at 17:00 (UTC+8). This airdrop will use a tiered airdrop model with high, medium, and low tiers. Users holding 136 ≤ Gate Alpha points ≤ 149 can receive 0.45 GT airdrop tokens, consuming 11 Gate Alpha points; users holding 150 ≤ Gate Alpha points ≤ 181 can receive 0.95 GT airdrop tokens, consuming 13 Gate Alpha points; and users holding ≥ 182 Gate Alpha points can receive 1.95 GT airdrop tokens, consuming 14 Gate Alpha points. Gate Alpha now supports popular public chains such as SOL, ETH, Gate Layer, BNB Chain, Base, SUI, ARB, World Chain, AVAX, Polygon, LINEA, ZK, OP, and Berachain. It also enables seamless trading of tokens across the entire chain through the contract address search function, opening up cross-chain transaction links and making all on-chain tokens available with a single click.

data.title: AIxCrypto launched on the AIxC Hub ecosystem platform, attracting over 200,000 users to participate in S1 Arena within five days.
data.description: On January 7th, Nasdaq-listed AIxCrypto (NASDAQ: AIXC) launched its AIxC Hub ecosystem engagement platform. Since the launch of its first season, S1 Arena, over 200,000 addresses have registered, with approximately 163,000 active. AIxC Hub employs a seasonal system and points mechanism, with all activities, points, rankings, and rewards calculated and settled on a seasonal basis. Around the EAI x RWA economic ecosystem and the Bestrade AI trading platform, it guides users to participate in market analysis and community interaction through a zero-capital simulation prediction mechanism. AIxC Hub will introduce more features and launch more marketing activities in the future.
